title: Architecture Evaluation, Quality Attributes, and Decision Governance · Decision Retrieval
summary: A delayed review reconstructs architecture evaluation, quality attributes, and decision governance from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for evaluated architecture and decision governance packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Describe context, containers, components, data, deployment, runtime, trust, operations, and ownership views at useful levels.
Analyze concrete quality-attribute scenarios and trade accessibility, security, privacy, reliability, performance, modifiability, cost, and simplicity explicitly.
Define failure domains, resource bounds, dependency timeouts, degraded modes, safe stop, recovery, and blast-radius evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review
steps.instruction:
review case — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

Inspect the Architecture Review trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review, keep Architecture Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Architecture Review, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Architecture Review precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Architecture Review, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Architecture review is a presentation seeking approval.

stimulus.title: Architecture Review case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The review variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Architecture review is a presentation seeking approval.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation. Artifact under review: evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Review, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Architecture Review record says evaluated architecture and decision governance packet looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Architecture Review.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Architecture Review without its stated preconditions, omit the evaluated architecture and decision governance packet trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Architecture Review for evaluated architecture and decision governance packet.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Architecture Viewpoints
steps.instruction:
Order the Architecture Viewpoints evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Architecture Viewpoints.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Architecture Viewpoints assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Architecture Review
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Architecture Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A box diagram omits quality tradeoffs, trust and failure boundaries, cost, evolution, dissent, review triggers, and rejected alternatives. The review variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Architecture Review.
steps.why: Retrieving Architecture Review through the concrete evaluated architecture and decision governance packet case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Architecture Review, keep Architecture Review connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run a structured review with scenarios, alternatives, evidence gaps, dissent, decisions, owners, and timed re-evaluation.
